inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 after-tax operating income $3700.0M ÷ 375.9M shares = $9.84/sh; excludes net realized gains per 10-K non-GAAP definition
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence base uses after-tax operating income (underwriting + investment spread), not peak GAAP net income ($4.4B) which includes realized gains and equity-method marks

bear.notes: Soft reinsurance pricing, cat-heavy year, mortgage claims rise; multiple compresses to ~9× normalized owner cash

base.notes: Mid-single-digit premium growth, combined ratios normalize, buybacks continue; 11× exit on year-10 owner cash

bull.notes: Specialty share gains persist, mortgage MI stays profitable, investment yields hold; sustained repurchases

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Investment portfolio $40B+ marked to fair value; goodwill from acquisitions; book $65/sh is cross-check not dhando floor (10-K FY2025)

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / dormant assets?
option_scan.evidence: Operating insurer/reinsurer; no land or royalty reserves

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Insurance segment combined ratio 95.2% near breakeven underwriting; reinsurance and mortgage carry group profits (10-K segment table)

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: Unearned premium reserve and renewal book embedded in premium growth assumptions; MCE acquisition integration in insurance segment

option_scan.question: Private or illiquid stakes below fair value?
option_scan.evidence: Equity-method investments $504M income FY2025; Coface trade-credit stake and fund investments marked per 10-K

option_scan.question: Transitory distribution / legal recovery?
option_scan.evidence: Earnings from underwriting and recurring investment income, not litigation recovery

option_scan.question: Embedded product option already in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Mortgage insurance and reinsurance premiums already in consolidated revenues $19.9B FY2025

growth_explanation.mechanism: Specialty insurance and reinsurance rate adequacy after 2022–2024 hard market; MCE acquisition expands U.S. mid-market platform; mortgage insurance benefits from housing activity and credit quality; investment float earns ~4% pre-tax yield.
growth_explanation.source: ACGL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260226_rpt20251231_acc0000947484_26_000017.htm segment underwriting table and net investment income disclosure
growth_explanation.bear_falsifier: Group combined ratio exceeds 100% for two consecutive years with no offsetting investment income
growth_explanation.bull_falsifier: Net premiums written grow faster than industry for four consecutive quarters while combined ratio stays below 90%

component_valuation.coverage_statement: Five additive components map insurance underwriting, reinsurance underwriting, mortgage insurance, investment float surplus, and catastrophe cycle reserve once each.

components.label: Insurance segment underwriting and investment spread

valuation.evidence: Primary bridge from FY2025 10-K: after-tax operating income $3700.0M ($9.84/sh), book $65.11/sh, net investment income $1625.0M, senior notes $2729.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K and Q1 2026 10-Q before decision use.
valuation.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

source.locator: Insurance segment underwriting income $375.0M; combined ratio 95.2% FY2025

inputs.label: After-tax operating income available to Arch common shareholders FY2025

source.locator: After-tax operating income $3700.0M FY2025 (non-GAAP, excludes net realized gains)

assumptions.label: Normalized after-tax operating income allocated to Insurance
assumptions.rationale: Insurance ~13% of group underwriting income; MCE acquisition adds mid-market scale.

assumptions.label: Growth years 1–5 on segment owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Lawrence scenario envelope; specialty insurance cycle normalization.

assumptions.label: Growth years 6–7 on segment owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Fade as premium base enlarges or mortgage origination slows.

assumptions.label: Required return on segment owner cash
assumptions.rationale: Underwriting, catastrophe, and investment-spread risk premium.

assumptions.label: Selling multiple in year 7
assumptions.rationale: Specialty insurer/reinsurer peer multiples on normalized owner cash.

assumptions.label: Component schedule reconciliation factor
assumptions.rationale: Preserves additive component schedule while filing facts anchor owner-cash bridge.

inputs.label: Total shareholders' equity available to Arch FY2025

Net investment income $1625.0M; pre-tax yield 4.11% on invested assets FY2025
Senior notes $2729.0M; senior notes to total capital 10.1% FY2025

assumptions.label: Investment float and marked portfolio surplus above segment owner-cash capitalization
assumptions.rationale: Float-backed investment portfolio earns $1.6B net investment income; modest senior notes ($2.7B) leave equity cushion; surplus not double-counted in segment DCF.

source.locator: Total net reserves $24,493M across insurance, reinsurance, and mortgage segments FY2025
Insurance combined ratio 95.2% FY2025 (94.8% prior year)

assumptions.label: Catastrophe, reserve development, and soft-market cycle stress reserve
assumptions.rationale: Negative reserve for property-cat events, social inflation, mortgage credit cycle, and reinsurance pricing normalization; not full net reserves double-count.

ownership_waterfall.net_economic_claim: One ACGL diluted share equals pro-rata insurance, reinsurance, and mortgage underwriting engines, investment float surplus, less catastrophe and cycle reserve.
excluded_claims: Net investment income already allocated proportionally through after-tax operating income in segment DCFs.
Full $24.5B loss reserves are not subtracted twice; catastrophe reserve captures stress claims only.
GAAP book ($65/sh) is cross-check, not dhando floor; economic value lives in normalized owner cash.
ownership_waterfall.reconciliation: FY2025 after-tax operating income $3700.0M on 375.9M shares ($9.84/sh); book value $65.11/sh; senior notes $2729.0M; net investment income $1625.0M.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of ACGL, including insurance, reinsurance, and mortgage underwriting engines, investment float surplus, and catastrophe cycle reserve.
economic_claim.unit_source: FY2025 weighted average diluted shares 375.9M per ACGL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260226_rpt20251231_acc0000947484_26_000017.htm.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating segments valued through owner-cash discount paths on allocated after-tax operating income; float surplus and stress reserve are separate overlap keys.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K: book value per share $65.11; after-tax operating income $3700.0M; economic value in normalized underwriting and investment spread, not book floor alone.

component_groups.label: Insurance segment underwriting engine
component_groups.economic_claim: U.S. and international specialty insurance platform
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on insurance share of after-tax operating income.
component_groups.adjustments: MCE acquisition (Allianz Aug 2024) expands mid-market platform; combined ratio 95.2%.

component_groups.label: Reinsurance segment underwriting engine
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on reinsurance share of after-tax operating income.
component_groups.adjustments: Combined ratio 80.8% FY2025; largest segment profit contributor.

component_groups.label: Mortgage insurance segment
component_groups.economic_claim: U.S. private mortgage insurance and risk transfer
component_groups.valuation_basis: Owner-cash discount on mortgage share of after-tax operating income.

component_groups.label: Investment float and portfolio surplus
component_groups.economic_claim: Marked investment portfolio and float economics above segment DCF
component_groups.valuation_basis: Net asset value on surplus investment income and equity cushion.

component_groups.label: Catastrophe and cycle stress reserve
component_groups.economic_claim: Property cat, social inflation, mortgage credit, and soft-market reserve
component_groups.valuation_basis: Bounded negative reserve; not full loss-reserve NAV subtraction.
component_groups.adjustments: Total net reserves $24.5B; reserve captures stress not base case.

limitations: Segment splits use underwriting-income-proportional after-tax operating income allocation.
Mortgage housing-cycle sensitivity remains widest judgment band.
